text.title: Laravel Framework Fundamentals Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for idiomatic Laravel framework usage.

rule.statement: Every contract (interface) MUST be bound to an implementation in a Service Provider's `register()` method. Inline `app()->bind(...)` in controllers/services is forbidden.

rationale.source: Laravel reference — service container + service providers

rule.rationale: Providers are loaded once at boot; inline bind is fragile and untestable.

rule.statement: Service classes MUST constructor-inject contracts (`__construct(private MailService $mail)`). Facades (`Mail::send`) MUST be restricted to controllers + artisan commands.
rule.rationale: Facades resolve from the container at call time — hard to mock without `Mail::fake()` boilerplate; constructor injection is testable with plain PHP.

rule.statement: Routes MUST be declared in `routes/*.php` files. Defining routes inside controllers, providers, or middleware is forbidden.
rule.rationale: `php artisan route:cache` requires routes in route files; inline routes break cache and observability tooling.

rule.statement: Configuration MUST be read via `config('group.key')` helper. `env()` calls outside `config/*.php` files are forbidden in production code.
rule.rationale: `env()` returns null after `config:cache`; only `config/*.php` files run before cache.

rule.statement: Cross-cutting concerns (auth check, CORS, rate limit, locale) MUST be implemented as middleware. Repeating the check in N controllers is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Middleware is the framework idiom; controller-level repetition drifts.
